channel.title: math.GN updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.GN updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Planar extensions in o-minimal structures
item.description: arXiv:2606.03155v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Let $X \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a closed definable set of dimension at most $1$, and let $h : X \to \mathbb{R}^2$ be a definable continuous injective map. In this paper, we establish necessary and sufficient combinatorial conditions, formulated in terms of cyclic orders at topological singular points and orientations of Jordan curves, for $h$ to admit a definable homeomorphic extension to the whole plane.
item.dc:creator: Dinh Si Tiep, Nhan Nguyen

item.title: Ends of stationary metric measure spaces
item.description: arXiv:2606.02864v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We prove that stationary random metric measure spaces have 0,1,2 or a Cantor space of ends. This notion includes stationary random graphs, manifolds and discrete subgroups. In the case of surfaces, we classify all possible homeomorphism types, in analogy with the work of Biringer and Raimbault on unimodular Riemannian manifolds. Our approach relies on a general "no geometric core" principle and an analysis of finite versus infinite expected return times.
item.dc:creator: Arie Levit, Kfir Silman

item.title: Homeomorphisms between compact subsets of real numbers
item.description: arXiv:2602.15542v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We first present a reduction of properties of compact sets of real numbers to properties of countable orders. Then discuss a variant of homogeneity of compact subsets of real numbers, focusing on the family of $t$-sets. Finally, we prove that there are exactly $\omega_1$ many non-homeomorphic $t$-sets.
item.dc:creator: S{\l}awomir Kusi\'nski, Szymon Plewik

item.title: ETH-Tight Complexity of Optimal Morse Matching on Bounded-Treewidth Complexes
item.description: arXiv:2603.05406v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: The Optimal Morse Matching (OMM) problem asks for a discrete gradient vector field on a simplicial complex that minimizes the number of critical simplices. It is NP-hard and has been studied extensively in heuristic, approximation, and parameterized complexity settings. Parameterized by treewidth $k$, OMM has long been known to be solvable on triangulations of $3$-manifolds in $2^{O(k^2)} n^{O(1)}$ time and in FPT time for triangulations of arbitrary manifolds, but the exact dependence on $k$ has remained an open question. We resolve this by giving a new $2^{O(k \log k)} n$-time algorithm for any finite regular CW complex, and show that no $2^{o(k \log k)} n^{O(1)}$-time algorithm exists unless the Exponential Time Hypothesis (ETH) fails.
item.arxiv:journal_reference: Proc. 42nd International Symposium on Computational Geometry (SoCG 2026), LIPIcs 367, 85:1--85:19, 2026
item.dc:creator: Geevarghese Philip, Erlend Raa V{\aa}gset
